publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-11-24 00:16:58 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Islamic Law and Society records
did.abstract: The Islamic Law and Society journal was co-founded in 1993 by Dr. David Powers, a professor of Near Eastern Studies at Cornell University, with Aharon Layish and Baber Johanson. The Islamic Law and Society records (1992-2022) contain administrative files, correspondence, article drafts and manuscripts, and reader responses to articles submitted for publication.
did.langmaterial: Most of the materials in this collection are in English. Some materials are in Hebrew, Arabic, French, Turkish, and German. Materials in languages other than English are indicated at the file level.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Islamic Law and Society records (1992-2022) contain administrative files, correspondence, article drafts and manuscripts, and reader responses to articles submitted for publication to Islamic Law and Society, an academic journal co-founded by Dr. David Powers, a professor of Near Eastern Studies at Cornell University, with Aharon Layish and Baber Johanson.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Islamic Law and Society journal was co-founded in 1993 by Dr. David Powers, a professor of Near Eastern Studies at Cornell University with Aharon Layish and Baber Johanson. The predominantly English-language journal provides a forum for research in the field of classical and modern Islamic law in Muslim and non-Muslim countries. The journal encourages discussion on all branches of Islamic law, with a view to promoting an understanding of Islamic law, in both theory and practice, from juridical, historical, and social-scientific perspectives.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Islamic Law and Society records, #14-30-4523. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections. Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Law.
International law.
Islamic law.
Islamic law--Periodicals.
Islamic law--Interpretation and construction.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Department of Near Eastern Studies

did.unittitle: Series I. Administrative files

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Candidates and terms of service for ILS Editorial Board. Includes lists of candidates, address, titles, and specialities.

did.unittitle: Meeting Agendas

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Meetings agendas for the first (1992) and second (1993) Editorial Meetings of the JILS.

Editorial Contracts and Agreements
Monthly Reports

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reviews of the journal in French and German.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials from the Schacht Conference for Theory and Practice in Islamic Law, October 1994.

Series II. Articles Submitted for Publication

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts, manuscripts, reader reviews, and correspondence regarding articles submitted for publication.

did.unittitle: Subseries IIa. Volumes 1-5

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Issue no. 3 titled "Gender, Family, and the Courts in Muslim Societies."

Vol. 5:2 Kahera, Plagarism accusation against

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding an accusation of plagarism in an article submitted by Akel Kahera to the journal titled "Informing the Act and Enacting the Form: A Mufti's use of a Legal Axiom in the 'Ordering of Habitat'." The article was later published as a co-authored piece titled, "Damages in Islamic Law: Maghribī Muftīs and the Built Environment (9th-15th Centuries C.E.)"

did.unittitle: Vol. 5:2 Schussman, Aviva, "The Legitimacy and Nature of Mawid al-Nabī: (Analysis of a Fatwā)"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in English, Arabic, and Hebrew.

Vol. 5:3 Carroll, Lucy, "Orphaned Grandchildren in Islamic Law of Succession: Reform and Islamization in Pakistan"

Vol. 15:2 Stewart, Devin, "The Students' Representative in the Law Colleges of 14th-Century Damascus"
Vol. 15:2 Opwis, Felicitas, "The Construction of Madhhab Authority: Ibn Taymiyya's Interpretation of Juristic Preference (Istiḥsān)"
Vol. 15:2 Ali, Mufti, "A Statistical Portrait of the Resistance to Logic by Sunni Muslim Scholars Based on the Works of Jalāl al-Dīn al-Suyūtī (849-909/1448-1505)"
Vol. 15:3 Lucas, Scott, "Where Are the Legal "Ḥadīth?" A Study of the "Muṣannaf" of Ibn Abī Shayba"
Vol. 15:3 Oberauer, Norbert, ""Fantastic Charities": The Transformation of "Waqf" Practice in Colonial Zanzibar"
Vol. 15:3 Weiss, Max, "Institutionalizing Sectarianism: The Lebanese Jaʿfari Court and Shiʿi Society under the French Mandate"
Subseries IId. Volumes 16-20
Vol. 16:1 Witztum, Joseph, "Quran 4:24 Revisited"
Vol. 16:1 March, Andrew, "Sources of Moral Obligation to non-Muslims in the "Jurisprudence of Muslim Minorities" (Ftqh al-aqalliyyāt) Discourse'"
Vol. 16:2 Kana'an, Ruba, "The de jure 'artist' of the Bobrinski Bucket: production and patronage of pre-Mongol metalwork and Khurasan and Transoxiana"
Vol. 16:2 Haider, Najam, "Prayer, Mosque, and Pilgrimage: Mapping Shīʿī Sectarian Identity in 2nd/8th Century Kūfa"
Vol. 16:2 Akgündüz, Ahmed, "Shariʿah Courts and Shariʿah Records: The Application of Islamic Law in the Ottoman State"
Vol. 16:2 Sayeed, Asma, "Gender and Legal Authority: An Examination of Early Juristic Opposition to Women's Ḥadīth Transmission"
Vol. 16:3 Hallaq, Wael, "Groundwork of the Moral Law: A New Look at the Qurʾān and the Genesis of Sharīʿa"
Vol. 16:3 Tillier, Mathieq, "Women before the Qāḍī under the Abbasids"
Vol. 16:3 Eich, Thomas, "Induced Miscarriage in Early Mālikī and Ḥanafī Fiqh"
Vol. 16:3 Ibrahim, Bilal, "Beyond State and Peasant: The Egalitarian Import of Juristic Revisions of Agrarian and Administrative Contracts in the Early Mamlūk Period"
Vol. 16:3 Hansu, Huseyin, "Notes on the Term Mutawātir and Its Reception in Ḥadīth Criticism"
Vol. 17:1 Rabb, Intisar, "Islamic Legal Maxims as Substantive Canons of Construction: Ḥudūd -Avoidance in Cases of Doubt"
Vol. 17:1 Pavlovitch, Pavel, "The Stoning of a Pregnant Adultress from Juhayna: the Early Evolution of a Muslim Tradition"
Vol. 17:2 Spevack, Aaron, "Apples and Oranges: The Logic of the Early and Later Arabic Logicians"
Vol. 17:2 Ergene, Boğaç, "Why Did Ümmü Gülsüm Go to Court? Ottoman Legal Practice between History and Anthropology"
Vol. 17:2 Cardinal, Monique, "Why Aren't Women Sharīʿa Court Judges? The Case of Syria"
Vol. 17:2 Mraja, Mohamed, "The Reform Ideas of Shaykh ʿAbdallāh Ṣāliḥ al-Farsī and the Transformation of Marital Practices among Digo Muslims of Kenya"
Vol. 17:3-4 Sadeghi, Behnam, "The Authenticity of Two 2nd/8th Century Ḥanafī Legal Texts: the Kitāb al-āthār and al-Muwaṭṭa' of Muḥammad b. al-Ḥasan al-Shaybānī"
Vol. 17:3-4 Burge, S.R., "Impurity/Danger!"
Vol. 17:3-4 Scheele, Judith, "Councils without Customs, Qadis without States: Property and Community in the Algerian Touat"
Vol. 17:3-4 Weimann, Gunnar, "Islamic Law and Muslim Governance in Northern Nigeria: Crimes against Life, Limb and Property in Sharīʿa Judicial Practice"
Vol. 18:1 Brown, Jonathan, "Even If It's Not True It's True: Using Unreliable Ḥadīths in Sunni Islam"
Vol. 18:1 Freidenreich, David, "The Implications of Unbelief: Tracing the Emergence of Distinctively Shiʿi Notions Regarding the Food and Impurity of Non-Muslims"
Vol. 18:1 Canbakal, Hulya, "Vows as Contract in Ottoman Public Life (17th-18th centuries)"
Vol. 18:2 Shaham, Ron, "Law versus Medical Science: Competition between Legal and Biological Paternity in an Egyptian Civil Court"
Vol. 18:2 Horii, Satoe, "Pre-emption and Private Land Ownership in Modern Egypt: No Revial of Islamic Legal Tradition"
Vol. 18:2 Fadel, Mohammed, "Is Historicism a Viable Strategy for Islamic Law Reform? The Case of 'Never Shall a Folk Prosper Who Have Appointed a Woman to Rule Them'"
Vol. 18:2 Shavit, Uriya and Ofir Winter, "Sports in Contemporary Islamic Law"
Vol. 18:3-4 Hallaq, Wael, "On Orientalism, Self Consciousness and History"
Vol. 18:3-4 Sartori, Paolo, "The Birth of a Custom: Nomads, Sharīʿa Courts and Established Practices in the Tashkent Province, ca. 1868-1919"
Vol. 18:3-4 Bernard-Maugiron, Nathalie, "Divorce and Remarriage of Orthodox Copts in Egypt: The 2008 State Council Ruling and the Amendment of the 1938 Personal Status Regulations"
Vol. 18:3-4 al-Atawneh, Muhammad, "Wahhābī Legal Theory as Reflected in Modern Official Saudi Fatwās: Ijtihād, Taqlīd, Sources, and Methodology"
Vol. 19:1-2 Motzki, Harald, "Theme issue: Methods of dating early legal traditions" and "Motzki's Reliable transmitter: A Short Answer to P. Gledhill"
Vol. 19:1-2 Yarbrough, Luke, "Upholding God's Rule: Early Muslim Juristic Opposition to the State Employment of non-Muslims"
Vol. 19:1-2 Pavlovitch, Pavel, "Some Sunni Ḥadīth on the Qurʾānic Term Kalāla: An Attempt at Historical Reconstruction"
Vol. 19:1-2 Gledhill, Paul, "Motzki's Forger: The Corpus of the Follower ʿAṭāʾ in Two Early 3 rd /9 th -Century Ḥadīth Compendia"
Vol. 19:3 Tillier, Mathieu, "Le cadi et le sauf-conduit (amān) : les enjeux juridiques de la diplomatie dans l'Orient abbasside"
Vol. 19:3 Omar, Sara, "From Semantics to Normative Law: Perceptions of Liwāṭ (Sodomy) and Siḥāq (Tribadism) in Islamic Jurisprudence (8 th -15 th Century CE)"
Vol. 19:3 Rubin, Avi, "Civil Disputes between the State and Individuals in the Ottoman Nizamiye Courts"
Vol. 19:3 Feener, R. Michael, "Social Engineering through Shaīʿa: Islamic Law and State-Directed Daʿwa in Contemporary Aceh"
Vol. 19:4 Shamsy & Zysow, "Al-Buwayṭīʾs Abridgment of al-Shāfiʿīʾs Risāla: Edition and Translation"
Vol. 19:4 Brown, Jonathan, "The Rules of Matn Criticism: There Are No Rules"
Vol. 19:4 al-Atawneh, Muhammad, "Leisure and Entertainment (malāhī) in Contemporary Islamic Legal Thought: Music and the Audio-Visual Media"
Vol. 19:4 Shavit, Uriya, "The Wasaṭī and Salafī Approaches to the Religious Law of Muslim Minorities"
Vol. 20:1-2 Oberauer, Norbert, "Early Doctrines on Waqf Revisited: The Evolution of Islamic Endowment Law in the 2 nd Century AH"
Vol. 20:1-2 Hideh, Najam, "Contesting Intoxication: Early Juristic Debates over the Lawfulness of Alcoholic Beverages"

Vol. 20:3 Nurlaelawati, Euis, "Muslim Women in Indonesian Religious Courts: Reform, Strategies, and Pronouncements of Divorce"
Vol. 20:3 Oba, Abdulmumini, "Judicial Practice in Islamic Family Law and Its Relation to ՙUrf (Custom) in Northern Nigeria"
Vol. 20:3 Shabana, Ayman, "Negation of Paternity in Islamic Law between Líՙān and DNA Fingerprinting"
Vol. 20:3 Spannaus, Nathan, "The Decline of the Ākhūnd and the Transformation of Islamic Law under the Russian Empire"
Vol. 20:4 Hendrickson, Jocelyn, "Is al-Andalus Different? Continuity as Contested, Constructed, and Performed across Three Mālikī "Fatwās""
Vol. 20:4 Hernandez, Rebecca Skreslet, "Sultan, Scholar, and Sufi: Authority and Power Relations in al-Suyūṭī's Fatwā on Waqf"
Vol. 20:4 Terem, Etty, "Redefining Islamic Tradition: Legal Interpretation as a medium for Innovation in the Making of Modern Morocco"
Subseries IIe. Volumes 21-25
Vol. 21:1-2 Benkheira, Mohammed, "L'impuissance sexuelle, motif légal de rupture du lien matrimonial: Genese d'une doctrine"

Vol. 23:4 Badareen, Nayel, "Shīʿī Marriage Law in the Pre-Modern Period: Who Decides for Women?"
Vol. 24:1-2 Frank, Allen, "Sharīʿa Debates and Fatwas among Nomads in Northern Kazakhstan, 1850-1931"
Vol. 24:1-2 Garipova, Rozaliya, "Married or not Married? On the Obligatory Registration of Muslim Marriages in Nineteenth-Century Russia"

Vol. 24:1-2 Ross, Danielle, "Muslim Charity under Russian Rule: "Waqƒ, Sadaqa," and "Zakat" in Imperial Russia"
Vol. 24:1-2 Saitori, Paolo, "Take Me to Khiva: Sharīʿa as Governance in the Oasis of Khorezm (19th–Early 20th Centuries)"
Vol. 24:3 Koujah, Rami, "Divine Purposiveness and its Implications in Legal Theory: The Interplay of "Kalām" and "Uṣūl al-Fiqh""

Vol. 24:4 Mian, Ali Altaf, "Troubling Technology: The Deobandi Debate on the Loudspeaker and Ritual Prayer"

Vol. 24:4 Thurston, Alexander, "Algeria's GIA: The First Major Armed Group to Fully Subordinate Jihadism to Salafism"
Vol. 25:1-2 Moumtaz, Nada, "Theme issue: A Third Wave of Waqf Studies" and ""Is the Family Waqf a Religious Institution?": Charity, Religion, and Economy in French Mandate Lebanon"

Vol. 25:4 Tillier, Mathieu and Naim Vanthieghen, "Un registre carcéral de la Fusṭāṭ abbasside"
Vol. 25:4 Oberauer, Norbert, "Money in Classical Islam: Legal Theory and Economic Practice"
Subseries IIf. Volumes 26-29
Vol 26:1-2 Stiles, Erin, ""It Is Your Right to Buy a Divorce": Judicial Khuluu in Zanzibar"

Vol. 26:3 Quadri, Junaid, "Moral Habituation in the Law: Rethinking the Ethics of the Sharīʿa"

Vol. 26:4 Dupret et al., "Filling Gaps in Legislation: The Use of Fiqh by Contemporary Courts in Morocco, Egypt, and Indonesia"
Vol. 26:4 Jones, Justin, "'Where Only Women May Judge': Developing Gender-Just Islamic Laws in India's All-Female 'Shari'ah Courts'"
Vol. 27:1-2 Weitz, Lev, "Islamic Law on the Provincial Margins: Christian Patrons and Muslim Notaries in Upper Egypt, 2nd-5th/8th-11th Centuries"
Vol. 27:1-2 Rock-Singer, Aaron, "Leading with a Fist: A History of the Salafi Beard in the 20th-Century Middle East"
Vol. 27:1-2 Belcher, Joel, "Scholars, Spice Traders, and Sultans: Arguing the Alms-Tax in the Mamluk Era"

Vol. 27:3 Keegan, Matthew Lindfors, "Levity Makes the Law: Islamic Legal Riddles"
Vol. 27:3 Bosanquet, Antonia Sigrid, "The kitabi Wife's Conversion to Islam. An Unusual Interpretation by Ibn Qayyim al-Jawziyya"
Vol. 27:3 Husayn, Nebil Ahmed, "Aḥkām concerning the ahl al-bayt"
Vol. 27:3 Mustafa, Abdul-Rahman, "Ritual and Rationality in Islam: A Case Study on Nail Polish"
Vol. 27:4 Van Den Bossche, Gowaart, "Destroying Churches by Performing Knowledge: Ibn al-Rifʿa's Kitāb al-nafā'is fī adillat hadm al-kanā'is (700/1301) and the Social Negotiation of Legal Authority"

Vol. 27:4 Halawi, Wissam Halabi, "Dhimmī-s de la Syrie rurale et institutions mameloukes: de l'utilisation de la théorie shāfiʿite à l'autonomie juridictionnelle du Patriarcat maronite d'après cinq actes d'achat inédits (IXe/XVe siècle)"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in English, Arabic, and French.

did.unittitle: Vol. 27:4 Fuchs, Simon Wolfgang, "Casting aside the Clutches of Conjecture: The Striving for Religious Certainty at Aligarh"
Vol. 27:4 Alatas, Ismail Fajrie, "From ʿAlid Treatise to anti-Shiʿi Text: the Riṣāla fī ibṭāl bidaʿ munkarāt of ʿAbdallāh b. ʿUmar Bin Yaḥyā (d. 1265/1849) and its Afterlife in Indonesia"
Vol. 28:1-2 Kirchner, Irene K. F., "Are Cryptocurrencies ḥalāl? On the Sharia-Compliancy of Blockchain-Based Fintech"
Vol. 28:1-2 Shafir, Nir, "Vernacular Legalism in the Ottoman Empire: Confession, Law, and Popular Politics in the Debate over the "Religion of Abraham (millet-i Ibrāhīm)""
Vol. 28:1-2 Soufi, Youcef L., ""Why Study Uṣūl al-Fiqh?": The Problem of Taqlīd and Tough Cases in 4th-5th /10th-11th Century Iraq"
Vol. 28:3 Anchassi, Omar, "Status Distinctions and Sartorial Difference: Slavery, Sexual Ethics, and the Social Logic of Veiling in Islamic Law"
Vol. 28:3 Oberauer, Norbert, "The Function of Documents in Islamic Court Procedure: a Multi-Dimensional Approach"

Vol. 29:3 Elhoudaiby, Ibrahim, "The Birth of the Islamic Investor"
Vol. 29:3, Abbasi, Muhammad Zubair, "Sexualization of Shari'a Application of Islamic Criminal (Hudud) Laws in Pakistan (1980-2018)"
Vol. 29:3 Amin, Nareman, "Rebellion against the Rules and the Ruler: Youth and Azhari Scholars' Authority in Post-2011 Egypt"
Vol. 29:4 Coetsee, Marillie, "Between Sale and Worship: Consistent Inconsistencies in Classical Hanafi and Maliki Rulings on Marital Annulments"
Vol. 29:4 Sheibani, Mariam, "Judicial Crisis in Damacus on the Eve of Baybars's Reform: The Case of the Minor Orphan Girl (651-55/1253-57)"
Vol. 29:4 Caeiro, Alexandre, "The Islamic Law of Pearling: Ritual Obligation and Economic Practice in the Arabian Gulf (1910s-1940s)"
